questions.text: Which AWS service provides a fully managed relational database?
questions.explanation: Amazon RDS (Relational Database Service) is a fully managed service that makes it easy to set up, operate, and scale relational databases in the cloud.

questions.text: What is the AWS shared responsibility model?

options.text: The customer is responsible for everything
AWS manages security OF the cloud; customers manage security IN the cloud
Security responsibilities are determined by the pricing tier

questions.explanation: Under the shared responsibility model, AWS is responsible for protecting the infrastructure (hardware, software, networking) while customers are responsible for securing their data, applications, and configurations.

questions.text: Which pricing model allows you to pay for compute capacity by the hour or second with no long-term commitments?
questions.explanation: On-Demand Instances let you pay for compute capacity by the hour or second with no long-term commitments or upfront payments.

questions.text: Which AWS service is used to deploy and manage containerized applications?
questions.explanation: Amazon Elastic Container Service (ECS) is a fully managed container orchestration service used to deploy and manage containerized applications.

questions.text: What does the AWS Well-Architected Framework provide?

options.text: A set of best practices and guidelines for building secure, high-performing, resilient cloud architectures

questions.explanation: The AWS Well-Architected Framework provides best practices across six pillars: Operational Excellence, Security, Reliability, Performance Efficiency, Cost Optimization, and Sustainability.

options.text: On-demand delivery of IT resources over the Internet with pay-as-you-go pricing
Storing files on a local hard drive
Running software only on physical servers in your office

questions.explanation: Cloud computing is the on-demand delivery of IT resources over the Internet with pay-as-you-go pricing, eliminating the need to buy, own, and maintain physical data centers.

questions.text: Which of the following is a benefit of cloud computing?

options.text: Trade upfront expense for variable expense

questions.explanation: One of the key benefits of cloud computing is trading upfront capital expenses (like buying servers) for variable operational expenses (pay only for what you use).

questions.text: What does 'elasticity' mean in cloud computing?

options.text: The ability to physically stretch server hardware
The ability to acquire resources as needed and release them when no longer required
The speed of the internet connection

questions.explanation: Elasticity refers to the ability to automatically scale computing resources up or down based on demand, ensuring you only use and pay for what you need.

questions.text: Which cloud deployment model runs applications entirely in the cloud?
questions.explanation: A cloud-based (public cloud) deployment runs all parts of the application in the cloud. Applications are either migrated to the cloud or designed and built directly in the cloud.

questions.text: What is the difference between IaaS, PaaS, and SaaS?

options.text: They represent different levels of cloud service abstraction: infrastructure, platform, and software
They are types of internet protocols

questions.explanation: IaaS (Infrastructure as a Service) provides basic building blocks. PaaS (Platform as a Service) removes the need to manage infrastructure. SaaS (Software as a Service) provides a complete product run by the provider.

questions.text: Which AWS service provides multi-factor authentication (MFA) for user accounts?
questions.explanation: AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) supports MFA, adding an extra layer of security by requiring users to provide a second form of authentication beyond their password.

questions.text: What is the principle of least privilege?

options.text: Granting only the minimum permissions needed to perform a task
Using the cheapest AWS services available
Reducing the number of AWS accounts

questions.explanation: The principle of least privilege means granting users and systems only the permissions they need to complete their tasks, reducing the potential impact of security breaches.

questions.text: Which AWS service automatically detects threats and monitors for malicious activity?
questions.explanation: Amazon GuardDuty is a threat detection service that continuously monitors for malicious activity and unauthorized behavior using machine learning, anomaly detection, and integrated threat intelligence.

options.text: Records API calls and user activity across your AWS account

questions.explanation: AWS CloudTrail records API calls made on your account, providing a history of AWS API calls for auditing, compliance monitoring, and operational troubleshooting.

questions.text: Which service helps you check whether your AWS environment follows security best practices?
questions.explanation: AWS Trusted Advisor inspects your AWS environment and provides recommendations across five categories: cost optimization, performance, security, fault tolerance, and service limits.
